mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.05 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during an easing cycle reflects improved manufacturing capacity utilization and corporate output, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns during documented easing periods, with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds the 195.0 index threshold, anticipated central bank tightening and margin compression force negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns remain positive in > 70% of observations where CPIAUCSL exceeds 195.0.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE produces a negative Rank IC over a 3-year rolling evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding economic output and corporate revenue fundamentals, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns (p-value > 0.10) across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts below 1.5 million units signals a sharp contraction in construction activity and consumer confidence, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of cases when HOUST drops below 1500.0.
